title: Self-Reliance
content:
Henceforth, please God, forever I forego
    The yoke of men's opinions. I will be
    Light-hearted as a bird, and live with God.
    I find him in the bottom of my heart,
    I hear continually his voice therein.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ralph Waldo Emerson, titled "Self-Reliance", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
